decision: Choose the default model configuration for English support-ticket routing.
selected_option: candidate-model with routing-prompt-v4 and no tools

measured_results.finding: Candidate route accuracy exceeded the baseline by 3.2 percentage points on identical frozen cases.
Neither configuration produced an unsafe-disclosure event.
Candidate mean latency was 11 percent higher but remained inside the recorded deployment limit.

judgment: The measured accuracy gain is large enough under the frozen rule to justify a guarded candidate rollout despite the latency increase.
tradeoffs: Prompt v4 becomes part of the deployed configuration and must be versioned with the model
cost_interpretation: The comparison uses marginal candidate request cost. Whole-session orchestration usage is reported separately and is not attributed to either model.
deployment_conditions: Start with a limited traffic share
Monitor safety, accuracy, latency, and drift
Rollback if any hard safety threshold fails
